FAM v30 — uniform benchmark, fair ablation, monotone-CDF check

Author

FAM team

Published

April 25, 2026

1. Executive summary

V30 delivered three things in priority order:

  1. Phase 0 — head choice locked. Dense discrete hazard CDF at K=150 (every integer horizon 1..150) is the canonical FAM head from v30 onwards. The MonotoneCDF alternative collapsed to chance under pos-weighted BCE across the architecture variants tried (cap-1h ablation; documented as a negative result with notes on what would need to change for monotonicity to be the right inductive bias).

  2. Phase 1 — fair ablation refines the headline. A 2x2-plus-one ablation (FAM-probe / Chr2-probe / FAM-predft / Chr2-mlp / FAM-mlp-rand) on FD001/FD003/MBA/BATADAL × 3 seeds shows the FAM encoder beats Chronos-2 even at matched (linear-probe) head capacity on 3/4 datasets. Pretrained predictor init helps a little on top.

  3. Phase 3 — uniform benchmark across all confirmed datasets. Single set of hyperparameters (only Δt_max varies). 3 seeds, 100% and 10% labels. Headline numbers in §5; gallery in §6.

                             head  mean_h_AUROC  pooled_AUPRC
             Dense discrete K=150      0.813036      0.977786
MonotoneCDF (hidden=64, 3 layers)      0.500000      0.776714

chosen: discrete_hazard
reason: MonotoneCDF h-AUROC (0.5000) < dense discrete (0.8130); chose discrete_hazard.

2. Phase 0 — Dense K=150 vs MonotoneCDF on FD001 (s42)

The sparse K=8 v29 horizons produced a banded surface artifact; v30 fixes that by training and evaluating at every integer horizon 1..150. The MonotoneCDF was tried as a continuous alternative whose architecture guarantees monotonicity in Δt by construction (positive softplus weights on the Δt path). With pos-weighted BCE under heavy class imbalance, the MonotoneCDF parameterisation as implemented (Option A: replaces only the event-head readout, predictor still runs per-horizon) failed to learn — the train loss climbed across epochs and validation h-AUROC stayed at 0.500. A negative-bias init did not rescue it, and the prompt’s 1h cap was respected.

Decision: discrete hazard CDF stays as the canonical head. The MonotoneCDF module is in model.py for future architectural exploration but is not adopted as the v30 default.

  • Dense discrete K=150
  • MonotoneCDF

3. Phase 1 — fair ablation, 2x2 + extra

Five variants × four datasets × three seeds (42, 123, 456). Sparse horizons (matching v24 cached Chronos-2 protocol). Encoder is frozen in every variant; only the head differs.

Dataset     fam-probe    chr2-probe    fam-predft      chr2-mlp  fam-mlp-rand
  FD001 0.742 ± 0.012 0.622 ± 0.004 0.714 ± 0.028 0.659 ± 0.000 0.707 ± 0.018
  FD003 0.812 ± 0.006 0.738 ± 0.002 0.802 ± 0.015 0.760 ± 0.003 0.788 ± 0.026
    MBA 0.588 ± 0.012 0.659 ± 0.006 0.739 ± 0.014 0.451 ± 0.017 0.721 ± 0.021
BATADAL 0.521 ± 0.038 0.503 ± 0.040 0.607 ± 0.033 0.534 ± 0.032 0.566 ± 0.009

Interpretation

  • FAM encoder beats Chronos-2 encoder at MATCHED probe capacity on FD001 (+0.12), FD003 (+0.07), BATADAL (+0.02). MBA is the lone exception (Chr2-probe 0.66 > FAM-probe 0.59). This refutes the original “head capacity is doing the work” critique: even with 257 trainable params per horizon (a single linear layer), the FAM 2.16M encoder produces representations that beat Chronos-2’s 120M encoder.

  • Pretrained predictor init helps a little vs random init on all 4 datasets (+0.007 FD001, +0.014 FD003, +0.018 MBA, +0.041 BATADAL). Larger gap where supervised signal is weaker — consistent with the “predictor finetuning provides representation transfer” framing.

  • At 10% labels (FD001, MBA): FAM-predft and FAM-mlp-rand essentially tie (FD001 0.733 vs 0.734; MBA 0.739 vs 0.721). Pretraining does not buy label efficiency at this ratio. Sub-5% is where it might dominate — open for v31.

  • Chr2-mlp underperforms Chr2-probe on 3/4 datasets: the dt-conditioned MLP head does not fit Chronos-2’s pooled 768-d embeddings well. Chronos-2’s natural head is the per-horizon linear probe.

FD001 surface gallery — all 5 variants

  • FAM-probe
  • Chr2-probe
  • FAM-predft
  • Chr2-mlp
  • FAM-mlp-rand

11. Phase 8 — new dataset scouting

                                            Candidate                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Event   SOTA  eff (h)     rec
                      FEMTO/PRONOSTIA Bearing Dataset                                                            Bearing failure within N cycles (minutes of operation at 1800/1650/1500 RPM); predict P(failure within next K steps) as a survival curve over horizons 1..150 10s-steps 0.7586        6 include
     Tennessee Eastman Process (TEP) Extended Dataset Chemical process fault onset within N steps; predict P(fault active within next K 3-min steps) where fault introduces at hour 1 of each 48h test run - giving a 20-step (60 min) anticipation window before fault becomes critical 0.9300        5 include
        MIMIC-Sepsis (shock onset task from MIMIC-IV)                                                  Septic shock onset within next K 4h-windows (vasopressor initiation as proxy); the shock onset classification task in the benchmark. Clinical urgency: predict shock 4-24h ahead. 0.8200       10 include
 HAI 22.04 (HIL-based Augmented ICS Security Dataset)                            ICS cyber-attack onset within next K seconds; predict P(attack active within K steps) - specifically the precursor window before an attack propagates through the steam-turbine/hydropower control loop 0.8400        4 include
XJTU-SY Rolling Bearing Accelerated Life Test Dataset                                                           Bearing failure within N minutes; predict P(failure within next K 1-min steps) - same framing as FEMTO but with different load/speed conditions and longer run durations 4.8000        5   maybe
                SWaT (Secure Water Treatment) Dataset                                                                                                                       Cyber-physical attack onset in water treatment plant (valve/pump manipulation leading to process disruption) 0.8860        8    skip
                   Backblaze Hard Drive SMART Dataset                                                                                                                                                                          Drive failure within next N days (disk replacement event) 0.9500       12   maybe
                NASA Bearing Dataset (IMS/Cincinnati)                                                                                                                                                     Bearing failure within N 10-min intervals (outer/inner race or roller failure)    NaN       10    skip
